text: This research investigates the psychological and behavioral development of students at SMP Nidaul Quran, Sarolangun, focusing on how discipline and religious values foster resilience and a sense of responsibility. Employing a qualitative case study design, data were collected through in-depth interviews with students, educators, and parents, supplemented by field observations and thematic document analysis. The findings reveal that discipline serves as a fundamental pillar for character building, moving beyond mere rule enforcement to facilitate the internalisation of integrity and self-control. The study highlights a successful synergy between behavioral standards and Islamic spiritual practices, such as congregational prayers and Quranic recitation, which strengthens students' moral fortitude and emotional resilience. Furthermore, the results underscore the vital role of teachers as moral exemplars and the necessity of active parental involvement in reinforcing school values at home. The study concludes that an integrated, holistic approach to Islamic education—incorporating structured discipline and spiritual cultivation—provides a robust framework for developing well-rounded individuals capable of navigating modern challenges with ethical integrity. This model offers scalable insights for other Islamic educational institutions seeking to balance academic competence with deep-seated character development.
